# function: pi_approximate_digits

Returns the first `count` decimal digits of 22/7 *following* the decimal point as a single ASCII string. Models the [RFC 3091 §1.1](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3091#section-1.1) TCP approximate service, which streams `"starting with the most significant digit following the decimal point"` — no seek operation, so this function takes only `count`.

Example:
- `pi_approximate_digits(12)` → `"142857142857"` (the 6-digit cycle, twice)

Because 22/7 is a period-6 repeating decimal, output for any count `c` is just `"142857"` repeated and truncated. Count is bounded only by what Go's `int` and your machine's memory can hold.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// pi_approximate_digits — RFC 3091 §1.1 TCP approximate service for 22/7. Returns the first `count` digits of 22/7 = 3.142857142857… (period-6).
output "two_cycles" {
  value = provider::burnham::pi_approximate_digits(12)
  // → "142857142857"
}

output "partial_cycle" {
  value = provider::burnham::pi_approximate_digits(5)
  // → "14285"
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
pi_approximate_digits(count number) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `count` (Number) How many digits to return; count >= 0. Empty string if count = 0.
